idea: EV Charger Network Expansion Pace Acceleration

data.source: OpenChargeMap API weekly charger count by state/region through openchargemap adapter

behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in available public EV chargers signals infrastructure investment confidence and forward demand expectations for electric vehicles.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger buildout is industrial capex spending and signals consumer EV adoption momentum, benefiting industrials and energy transition plays.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly new charger count exceeds 200-unit increase over prior week average, triggering bullish infrastructure signal
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly charger additions fall back below 100-unit threshold

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger networks expand in spurts tied to state/federal incentive cycles; growth accelerations occur multiple times per quarter.

idea: Google Trends Spike in Appliance Shortage Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' + 'appliance delivery delay' through google_trends adapter

behavior: Sharp spikes in appliance shortage and delivery delay searches indicate supply chain friction and consumer frustration, signaling imminent price pressures and margin compression.

market_impact.rationale: Appliance shortage spikes foreshadow consumer discretionary spending pullback and retail margin pressure.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 2-week rolling average by 40% or more
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when trend volume falls below 20% above rolling average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain volatility creates recurring appliance shortage search spikes; typical fire rate 2-4 times per quarter.

idea: Freight Density Index via Google Trends Logistics Job Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'truck driver job hiring' + 'logistics coordinator job' through google_trends adapter

behavior: Elevated logistics job search volume reflects tight freight capacity and rising demand for shipping services, signaling inflationary pressure on transport costs.

market_impact.rationale: Rising logistics job search volume correlates with freight rate spikes and industrial transport demand strength.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume for logistics job searches exceeds 3-week average by 35%
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if trend falls below rolling average by 10%

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and cyclical freight demand creates recurring spikes in job search volume; fires 2-3 times per month on average.

idea: Healthcare Cost Shock via Google Trends Medical Bill Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'hospital bill negotiation' through google_trends adapter

behavior: Spikes in medical bill and payment plan searches indicate consumer healthcare cost shock, signaling financial distress in lower-income cohorts.

market_impact.rationale: Healthcare cost shock reduces discretionary spending capacity and pressures consumer staples and retail margins.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 4-week rolling average by 50%
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when trend normalizes below 25% above rolling average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Medical billing cycles and insurance deductible resets create recurring spikes; typical frequency 3-4 times per quarter.

idea: Rental Market Heat via Google Trends Apartment Search Surge

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'apartment rentals near me' + 'rent prices' through google_trends adapter

behavior: Spikes in apartment rental and rent price searches indicate housing market tightness and inflation in residential costs, signaling wealth squeeze.

market_impact.rationale: Rising rental cost pressure reduces discretionary income and consumer spending capacity.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume for apartment searches exceeds 3-week average by 45%
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or if trend falls back to rolling average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal rental cycles (spring/summer, year-end) create predictable spikes; fires 3-5 times per quarter.

idea: Cybersecurity Crisis Sentiment via News RSS Feed Volume

data.source: RSS feed article count weekly from major tech/security news sources (Ars Technica, SecurityWeek, Dark Reading) filtered for 'zero-day' + 'vulnerability' + 'breach' keywords through rss_count adapter

behavior: Spikes in cybersecurity breach and vulnerability news article counts signal heightened security incident activity, triggering defensive rotation and IT spending urgency.

market_impact.rationale: Cybersecurity crises drive emergency IT and security software budget allocation, benefiting tech security vendors.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly article count from security RSS feeds exceeds 2-week average by 60%
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if article count drops below 30% above average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly cybersecurity incidents and vulnerability disclosures occur constantly; fire rate typically 2-3 times per week.

idea: Warehouse Automation Demand via Google Trends Robotics Job Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'warehouse robotics job' + 'automation technician hiring' through google_trends adapter

behavior: Rising robotics and automation job search volume indicates accelerating warehouse automation capex cycles and labor replacement trends.

market_impact.rationale: Warehouse automation investments drive industrial equipment and robotics company revenue growth.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 4-week average by 40%
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when trend falls below rolling average by 15%

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: E-commerce volume and holiday season cycles drive quarterly automation capex waves; fires 2-4 times per quarter.

idea: Airline Load Factor Drop Signal via BTS Proxy Data

data.source: BTS monthly airline load factor (Revenue Passenger-Miles / Available Seat-Miles) through bts_airline_load_factor adapter

behavior: Declining airline load factors indicate weakening travel demand and reduced pricing power, signaling consumer discretionary spending pullback.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor declines correlate with consumer leisure travel pullback and discretionary income squeeze.

trade_logic.entry: If 4-week rolling load factor drops 2% or more below prior 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or when load factor stabilizes above prior average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings create regular load factor oscillations; typical fire rate 2-3 times per quarter.

idea: Commodity Input Pressure via FRED PPI Spike

data.source: FRED series: Producer Price Index for Crude Materials (PPICRM) weekly through fred_series adapter

behavior: Weekly spikes in crude materials PPI indicate upstream cost inflation, signaling margin pressure on industrials and energy-intensive sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Rising raw material costs compress manufacturing margins and signal inflationary headwinds.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly PPI reading exceeds 4-week rolling average by 1.5% or more
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when PPI normalizes within 0.5% of rolling average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity prices fluctuate weekly; PPI spikes occur 2-4 times per month during volatile periods.

idea: Earthquake Insurance Demand Surge via Google Trends

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'earthquake insurance' + 'home insurance after earthquake' through google_trends adapter, cross-referenced with USGS earthquake activity feed through earthquake_activity adapter

behavior: Spikes in earthquake insurance searches following seismic activity indicate regional economic shock and risk re-evaluation, signaling local property market repricing.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake insurance demand spikes benefit insurance and reinsurance company underwriting revenue and premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If USGS detects 4.0+ magnitude earthquake in populated region AND Google Trends volume for earthquake insurance exceeds 3-week average by 70% within 2 days
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days or if search volume normalizes

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity in North America and Pacific regions occurs regularly; significant earthquakes trigger insurance search spikes 3-5 times per year, with weekly opportunities.
